rationale: The citing paper uses Ghosh and John (2009) in its core theorizing that governance mechanism effects are contingent on contextual attributes, citing it when introducing HQ-subsidiary contingencies. It cites the same work again in discussion/conclusion to frame findings as evidence of selective alignment between control mechanisms and firm characteristics with economic consequences. This is more than a passing background mention, even though the citation is often grouped with others. The role is conceptual framing rather than direct reuse of the cited paper's empirical setting or method.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the governance-value/alignment logic associated with Ghosh and John (2009) to a different setting, arguing that MNC subsidiary control mechanisms work differently depending on HQ-subsidiary relationship conditions. It then interprets its results through that governance-fit lens.
